description: REQ-EDIT-035 'editing+dirty' branch implementation discards the user's intent. Per spec REQ-EDIT-035 / PROP-EDIT-024a: 'RequestNewNote while editing AND isDirty=true dispatches TriggerBlurSave first; RequestNewNote is not dispatched until the snapshot leaves saving.' The handler dispatches `dispatchTriggerBlurSave` and then `return;` — RequestNewNote is silently dropped, never deferred. There is no queued intent, no $effect waiting on the saving→editing transition to fire RequestNewNote. So after the save completes, the user remains on the same note instead of getting a new one. The PROP-EDIT-024a vitest only asserts `dispatchRequestNewNote.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(0)` immediately after the click — it never simulates the saving→editing snapshot transition to verify RequestNewNote eventually fires. The 'is not dispatched until the snapshot leaves saving' clause of the spec is unverified and unimplemented.
suggestedRemediation: Set a pending flag (`let pendingNewNoteSource = $state<NewNoteSource | null>(null)`); in the editing+dirty branch, set `pendingNewNoteSource = source` then dispatch TriggerBlurSave. Add a `$effect` that watches the saving→editing transition (lastSaveResult==='success', isDirty===false) and, if `pendingNewNoteSource !== null`, dispatches RequestNewNote and clears the pending flag. Add an integration test that drives the full sequence: click → TriggerBlurSave fires → emit `{status:'saving'}` → emit `{status:'editing', isDirty:false, lastSaveResult:'success'}` → assert RequestNewNote fires.
